data.title: BlackBerry beats Q2 earnings estimates with $0.04 per share, revenue of $129.6M, expects $132M-$140M sales in Q3.
data.description: BlackBerry Limited (NYSE: BB) reported positive second-quarter results with earnings of $0.04 per share, surpassing the estimated $0.01. Quarterly revenue stood at $129.6 million, marking a 3% increase from last year and exceeding the expected $122.1 million. For the upcoming third quarter, BlackBerry anticipates earnings per share in the range of 2 to 4 cents, compared to the consensus of 3 cents. Sales are projected to fall between $132 million and $140 million, while the consensus was at $137.73 million. The company also expects adjusted earnings per share for FY26 to be between 11 and 15 cents.

data.title: Bitcoin price remains unaffected by PCE inflation data, as support at $109K is broken with bid liquidity at $108,200.
data.description: Following the release of significant US inflation data, Bitcoin did not demonstrate a significant recovery as bid liquidity remained concentrated around $108,200.

data.title: ITS Logistics released September Supply Chain Report noting U.S. economy signals mixed; Containerized imports at top 10 U.S. ports fell 4.1% in August 2025; De minimis exemption ended on August 29 for imports in the U.S., impacting e-commerce peak season plans.
data.description: In the September ITS Supply Chain Report, ITS Logistics highlighted the U.S. economy's mixed signals, with positive growth and consumer spending but stubbornly high inflation above target. No recession seems imminent, but economic momentum is slowing heading into Q4, causing rising uncertainty. Professionals are gearing up for the domestic logistics peak season by reassessing strategies related to tariff arbitrage, de minimis opportunities, warehouse locations, and the trucking sector's capacity gains. Containerized imports at the top 10 U.S. ports fell 4.1% in August 2025, with declines at key West Coast gateways offset by gains at Seattle, Savannah, and Norfolk. Import volumes are above pre-pandemic levels but sensitive to policy changes like the recent repeal of the de minimis exemption, causing challenges for e-commerce supply chains during the peak season. The exemption, which allowed duty- and tax-free entry for imports under $800, was eliminated to combat drug trafficking and tariff avoidance. Companies are now struggling to adjust operations for the Q4 holiday shopping rush. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Vietnam prioritizes AI as economic driver, showcased at NVIDIA AI Day in Ho Chi Minh City, aiming to excel in AI in Southeast Asia.
data.description: Vietnam has emphasized the importance of artificial intelligence (AI) in its economic strategy, as showcased at NVIDIA AI Day in Ho Chi Minh City. The country aims to establish itself as a prominent AI nation in Southeast Asia. This strategic focus on AI underscores Vietnam's commitment to leveraging cutting-edge technologies for economic advancement and innovation.

data.title: XPPen introduces Artist Ultra 16 with 4K OLED screen, X-Touch control, and 16K pressure levels. Los Angeles, Sept.26, 2025.
data.description: XPPen, a prominent player in digital art advancement, introduced the Artist Ultra 16 in Los Angeles on September 26, 2025. This cutting-edge drawing display marks a significant milestone as the first of its kind to feature a 4K OLED screen, the groundbreaking X-Touch interactive control system, and an impressive 16K pressure levels technology. This innovative device is setting new standards in the industry, offering artists unparalleled precision and control.

data.title: Gofest in Bogotá, Colombia becomes premier entrepreneurship festival with 24,000 attendees, 200 talks, 280 investors, 300 speakers, and 70 Family Offices.
data.description: In Bogota, Colombia, as of September 26, 2025, an event took place with a massive turnout of 24,000 attendees. The event featured approximately 200 talks, 280 investors, 300 speakers, and 70 Family Offices. Moreover, there were 1,000 business meetings conducted among startups, investors, and corporates. The event also saw participation from over 250 entrepreneurs showcasing their top offerings. This gathering was hosted by the Bogotá Chamber.

data.title: EMC testing market expected to reach $4.48B by 2031 with 6.8% CAGR, driven by consumer electronics.
data.description: A recent detailed report by The Insight Partners has brought attention to the increasing growth of the EMC testing market. In this comprehensive analysis of the market, various aspects including market size, emerging trends, and the key players in the industry have been outlined. The report also reveals strategic opportunities for the market.

data.title: World premiere of "Finding Titanic: The Secret Mission" opens on September 27, 2025 at Peoria Riverfront Museum with discoverer Dr. Robert Ballard.
data.description: Renowned ocean explorer Dr. Robert Ballard, known for discovering the Titanic, recently revealed new insights about the famous shipwreck in Peoria, Illinois. On September 26, 2025, Dr. Ballard shared his findings at a museum in Peoria. The collaboration with Dr. Ballard provided valuable information about the RMS Titanic, captivating audiences with first-hand accounts of the discovery. Attendees had the unique opportunity to hear from the expert himself as he unveiled previously undisclosed details. This event in Peoria attracted a significant crowd eager to learn more about the historic shipwreck, highlighting the ongoing fascination with the Titanic saga.

data.title: Balavant Insurance Group names Michael Sothen as CFO and Omeed Kazemi as COO to expand leadership team.
data.description: Today, Novato-based Balavant Insurance Group revealed two key appointments to its leadership team. Michael Sothen has been appointed as the company's Chief Financial Officer, while Omeed Kazemi will take on the role of Chief Operating Officer. The appointments were made in an effort to enhance the company's strategic direction. Sothen brings a wealth of financial experience to his new role, while Kazemi will oversee the company's operational functions. These appointments come amidst a period of growth for Balavant, as it continues to strengthen its position within the industry.

data.title: Factor Bioscience files complaint against Cellectis and AstraZeneca for infringing three U.S. gene-editing patents crucial for cancer therapy.
data.description: Three U.S. gene-editing patents have been allegedly infringed, with the patents supporting various product pipelines, such as Factor's allogeneic cancer cell therapy programs. The company emphasizes the necessity of taking action to safeguard Factor's capacity to deliver cutting-edge cancer treatments to patients. This development underscores the importance of protecting intellectual property rights in the biotech sector.

data.title: Georgia Power anniversary of Hurricane Helene highlights importance of severe weather prep. Mobilized largest restoration op; most destructive storm in history.2025 Atlantic Hurricane Season ongoing.
data.description: A year ago, Georgia Power initiated its most extensive restoration effort to date to address the aftermath of the most devastating storm in the company's records. The 2025 Atlantic Hurricane Season is ongoing, with the potential development of storms that may affect Coastal Georgia. This was reported from Atlanta on September 26, 2025.
